desc:
A narrow footpath leads from the western plains into a thicket of thornbushes to
the east. Layer upon layer of thorny branches line both sides of the almost
invisible path. Shifting gaps in the thorny tangle to the north reveal snippets
of a small, black tower.
name: Twisted Path

desc:
A small tower is carved into the granite cliff here, its black stonework covered
with thick moss and twisting vines. Dry ground and patches of dead grass lead
towards the entrance, a wide arch of chiselled stone. Several crude fetishes are
hung from the arch, and some strange symbols have been scratched into it. A dark
doorway leads into the tower.
name: At a Black Tower

desc:
A small forest of yellow-green ash trees provides a bright backdrop against a
featureless, grey cliff-face to the east. A thin covering of hilbert and filbert
grows beneath the trees. A black wall of writhing thorns turns, and vibrates
against the grey stone. The ground here is soft and spongy, as the decaying
leaves provide a brown lattice on the hilly ground.
name: Light Brush between Hills

desc:
A large gash runs the length of the rocky hill here. Small shrubs grow on each
side of it, obscuring the dark trap from unwatchful travellers. The rolling
hills continue to the northwest, and south. Far to the southwest, a lush,
multicoloured brush lines a vast basin.
name: Broken Hill above Light Brush

desc:
Grass and small bushes grow among the dirt and rock in small clumps, and the
ground here is mostly smooth, with occasional bumps of large rock. To the north,
a long ridge reaches outward from a granite crag, casting a small part of the
ground below it in unending shadow. Twin cracks have formed under it, snaking
their way to the grassy ground.
name: Below a Granite Ridge

desc:
The rocky, barren hill here gives way to a collage of green, brown, and yellow,
as a dense basin of brush opens below to the west. A small family of rolling
hills is clustered to the north and northwest. A small, black cave opening dots
the grey cliff to the east.
name: Barren Hills

desc:
The flat field, unmarked by boulders or trees, ends abruptly with a steep
crevice to the south, which is filled with mounds of rubble. To the west and
south, the only visible landmarks are the long ridges of the Northern Downs,
leading lost travellers southward towards the city of Fornost.
name: Flat Field

desc:
The northwestern corner of the crag has completely collapsed, leaving a huge
rockslide to the north, and a shallower slope to the west. To the south, the
ridge still holds fast against the constant barrage of winds. A few shrubs and
tufts of grass are still somehow clinging to the rocky ground.
name: Crumbling Ridge

desc:
The sharp ridgeline here angles upward, until it resembles a rocky blade. Atop
the ridge, a flat outcrop has a small, nest-like niche worn into it by the
weather. To the south, the Northern Downs continue until meeting the flat plains
of Fornost. A small stone structure can be seen, nestled inside a thicket to the
southeast.
name: Stony Overlook

desc:
The granite cliffs squeeze on all sides here, funnelling travellers to a sudden
stop at the eastern rock wall. A large mound of sharp, grey rocks, tree limbs,
and detritus is heaped onto the wall. Large patches of furry green moss cling to
sky-facing sides of the rock pile, and saplings sprout from clumps of dirt in
its depths. Tiny animals scurry within the safety of its dark interior.
name: Giant Rockslide

desc:
The grey and white landscape offers little place to shelter from the wind, as
only a few elongated niches in the rock provide substantial cover. Vegetation is
scarce, consisting of patches of grass, woody bushes, and short vines. Grey and
white pebbles lay scattered across the stony hilltop here, creating a slippery
mat for unsuspecting travellers.
name: Pebbly Field

desc:
A large pile of grey boulders, intermixed with crumbling branches and leaves,
clogs the entrance of this small cave. A small depression at the top of the pile
allows traversal through it. The dirt walls here are straight and smooth, and
rusting iron rails run parallel on the floor. Near the walls, giant wood beams
run the height of the room, forming structure for this dig.
name: Abandoned Mining Tunnel

desc:
Iron rails caked with rust run east-west through the tunnel, disappearing under
a giant rockfall further within the cave. A pair of skeletal legs poke out from
within the mound, the bones bleached white with age. Next to the legs, a jumble
of wood and iron, perhaps the remnants of a mining cart, lays within the rocky
cage.
name: Caved-in Mining Tunnel

desc:
Pine and ash trees form a light canopy over the uneven, rocky terrain, allowing
ample sunlight to reach the lush undergrowth below it. The terrain rises sharply
to the north, as a series of rolling hills emerges from the flat landscape. The
trail quickly disappears into the thick undergrowth to the west.
name: Beaten Trail through Rocky Underbrush

desc:
This small cave in the hillside is thick with the musty scent of wild animal,
who must have made this their home. Scraps of fur and a single, gnawed bone make
this all the more clear. The only exit is via a small hole in the roof of the
cave.
name: Musty Cave

desc:
The edge of the brush meets the edge of a cliff, with trees ending at a sheer
and quite impassable drop to the south. The edge is a fine vantage point, giving
a view of a long valley running far to the south. By contrast, the view is
obscured to the north, where bushes grow denser. Rough plains stretch out to the
west.
name: Scrub above a Valley

desc:
The narrow valley runs north-south, bounded by steep slopes. To the west climbs
a steep yet traversable slope, while to the east a sheer cliff looms upwards for
hundreds of feet. Erosion and continued rockfalls have covered the ground in
small rocks and stones, but also made for a passable way up.
name: Rocky Valley

desc:
The valley comes to an abrupt end here at the base of an almost sheer cliff.
Around the base of the cliff are large piles of rocks and boulders that have
accumulated from rockfalls in the past. The sides of the valley are too steep to
climb at this point, so the only choice is to leave via the south.
name: Below a Cliff

desc:
The bottom of this dip in the downs runs north-south. Rocks and boulders litter
the ground, some seeming to have fallen recently from above. To the east, the
hillside rises sharply again, making progress in that direction impossible. The
valley is strangely quiet, the only sound being the occasional pebble falling
from above.
name: Secluded Valley

desc:
The valley comes to an abrupt end here, with a steep drop to the river below. A
small patch of ground has been cultivated to form a small, secluded garden in
which grow various strange and unusual plants. A calm serenity fills this place,
in part owing to the muted roar of a nearby waterfall.
name: Small Garden above a River

desc:
This mossy-walled cave appears to be a natural formation in the hillside. Water
drips occasionally from the low ceiling, making the only sound in this otherwise
silent place. Scraps of war-gear lie abandoned on the floor, much of it
unusable, evidence perhaps of a deserter, or some warrior embracing a more
peaceful life.
name: Mossy Cave

desc:
The thick bushes come abruptly to an end here at the top of a steep muddy bank.
At the bottom of the drop lies a swift-running river, and from the roar of the
water and the spray a large waterfall must lie further to the north. To the
west, a large mound rises up from under some brambles, while to the south the
brush thickens.
name: Edge of the Brush

desc:
This large, warm cavern is thick with the smell of decomposed bodies and damp
leaves. Several carcasses litter the floor, some crudely pulled apart, and all
showing extensive signs of gnawing. The only exit is a dark, narrow tunnel out
towards the bushes beyond.
name: Wolves' Den

desc:
A small opening in the granite wall gradually opens into a wide room of slick,
dark stone. Little direct sunlight reaches the interior of this cave, and
bulbous mushrooms sprout along its curved walls. The sloped floor here is
covered in a thin layer of clear slime, and in the corner, a doline drops into
the deep recesses of the mountain. A clump of dark fur lies in a heap next to
the doline, and a crumbled mass of bones and sinew peeks out from it.
name: Dim Cave in the Hills

desc:
Water drips from the ceiling of this large cavern and falls on the floor forming
large puddles in several places. To the south a loud roaring sound can be heard
while to the west, a tunnel entrance is dimly visible in the cavern wall.
name: Behind a Waterfall

desc:
This is a narrow stone tunnel that appears to be part natural, part man-made.
Perhaps this was once an underground waterway that was at some time enlarged to
make a traversable tunnel. The tunnel winds around from the east to the north.
name: Watery Tunnel

desc:
Here the narrow, stone tunnel splits in two directions, one going north and one
going west. The only sound is the incessant dripping of water as it falls from
the ceiling to land in numerous small puddles on the floor. The faint sound of
water is heard to the south.
name: Tunnel Intersection

desc:
The tunnel ends abruptly at a dead end at this point, with further progress
blocked by a sheer rock wall. At the base of the wall is a large dark pool from
which a strange smell emanates. The exit east leads towards the darkness of a
long tunnel.
name: Dead End Tunnel

desc:
The rough, stone walls of this narrow tunnel runs approximately north-south. The
dripping of water is a faint, constant sound as it falls from the ceiling to
land in numerous small, dark puddles on the floor. The damp ground bears the
sign of old footprints.
name: Damp Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel opens out into a small cave at this point, one that bears the sign of
active habitation. There are bits of junk and refuse that have been piled up in
one corner, with the barest semblance of order. In the north wall of the cave is
a small opening.
name: Small Cave

desc:
This is a small cave, crowded with tables and chairs. There are various pieces
of junk and useless items stacked around the walls. The ceiling is low, and
lanterns dangle from it, providing light. A couple of low beds are standing
against the walls. To the south there is a small cave, and to the west there is
a lit passageway.
name: Cramped Cave

desc:
The narrow stone passageway bends between the east and north. Lanterns are
attached to the walls with crude fixtures, and the absence of dust makes it
obvious that this passage is used fairly often. In the dim, flickering light,
openings into small rooms to the north and east are visible.
name: Lit Passageway

desc:
This is a large cave with a straw-covered floor and a low ceiling. The cave is
lit by several torches along the walls, casting shadows across piles of refuse
and junk in the corners of the room. Amidst these signs of habitation is a
narrow hatchway, leading down.
name: Main Den Room

desc:
Judging by the boxes and barrels that have been strewn all over the floor, this
small cave is being used as a storage room. Despite the disorder, the lack of
dust over the room's contents suggests this room has not been left idle. The way
out is a sturdy ladder leading upwards, towards a lit open cavern.
name: Storage Room

desc:
The ground floor of this circular tower sports a spiral stone staircase that
leads to the floors above and below. The flooring is made of rough stone blocks,
and the curved walls are painted black. On the floor lies a circular red carpet,
embossed with a green fire breathing dragon.
name: Entrance Hall of a Small Tower

desc:
This damp cellar is cluttered with old books, soot-covered bottles, musty
garments, and rotting bodies. The buckled floorboards are coated in a fine layer
of slime, and the fragrant stench of decay permeates the entire room. A circular
stone staircase leads upwards into the castle.
name: Tower Cellar

desc:
A large black cauldron steams in the middle of this floor, rippling the air
above it. Smells of strong spices, and rotting meat, emanate from it. On the
southern wall, a few cupboards store bread, vegetables, and spices. A spiral
stone staircase provides access to upper and lower floors of the tower.
name: Tower Kitchen

desc:
This room contains a few chairs, a small oak table, and a fine oak bed. Two
grizzly bear carpets provide warmth on the cold stone floor. Through a window to
the south, a rough landscape of small hills can be seen. A trapdoor leads up to
the roof, via a ladder. A spiral staircase leads down.
name: Living Chamber in a Black Tower

desc:
An iron fence, topped with red spikes, surrounds the flat roof of this small
tower. Below, to the south, rippling masses of black thorns surround a tight
path that leads to this castle. Past the thorns, to the northwest, the rolling
hills of the Northern Downs cover the landscape.
name: Roof of a Black Tower

desc:
The river abruptly ends here, flowing off the top of a waterfall as it carries
on its journey west. The rushing noise of the water fills the air, accompanying
a majestic view of the river and plains to the west. To the south, the brushland
stands as a thick border to the river, with thin branches grasping out over the
flowing surface of the water. Following the river into open air is possible, but
perilous.
name: At the top of a Roaring Waterfall

desc:
A thick mist of water spray permeates the air here making it impossible to
distinguish anything more than a few feet away. The sound of falling water is so
loud as to drown out any other noise. The turbulent waters of the river, which
roar down from a cliff to the east, buffet anyone trying to maintain their
position standing here.
name: Under a Waterfall

desc:
The rushing curtain of water crashes against the cliff face as it pours from the
river above towards a rapidly-approaching shallow basin, which lies a mere dozen
feet from the top of the waterfall. As the torrent of water falls all around, it
blocks any sight of the surrounding wilderness.
name: Falling down the Waterfall
